rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR VS. SHRYNE GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Second Amended Complaint; Declaration; And, Memorandum.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Monday, March 10, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO's Motion To Amend Second Amended Complaint. Off calendar per 3/3/25 order of dismissal of entire case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SHASE LELAND HERNANDEZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO CONSERVATORY OF MUSIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Monday, March 10, 2025, Line 9. HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED JUDGMENT. Hearing required. rulings.Case Title: DANISHIA DAVID VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Monday, March 10, 2025, Line 7. 1 - DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Production Of Documents. Defendants' motion to compel Plaintiff to respond to Chinatown Community Development Center's (CCDC) Requests for Production of Documents, Set Two, is granted. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2031.300(a).) Plaintiff is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents within 10 court days of entry of this order. Defendants also seek sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Considering all the circumstances here including that Plaintiff is self-represented, the court does not find substantial justification and awards sanctions of $1000 payable by Plaintiff to CCDC. Plaintiff shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANISHIA DAVID VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Monday, March 10, 2025, Line 7. 4 - DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions. Defendants' motion to compel Plaintiff to appear for deposition is granted. The Hollenbeck declaration submitted with the motion demonstrates that Defendants have repeatedly tried to procure Plaintiff's deposition and have served a notice of deposition, most recently on October 15, 2024. Plaintiff's then-counsel objected to the noticed date but did not provide an alternative date. The court finds Defendants' meet and confer efforts to be reasonable but notes that Plaintiff has been self-represented since January 22, 2025. Pursuant to CCP 2025.450(a), the court orders Plaintiff to appear for deposition within 15 court days of entry of this order unless the parties mutually agree to a later date. Plaintiff shall meet and confer with Defendants' counsel within five court days of entry of this order to set the deposition date. Defendants also seek sanctions. Because Plaintiff is now self-represented and there is no evidence before the court that Defendants have communicated with Plaintiff to set her deposition since her prior counsel was relieved, the court finds that the imposition of sanctions would be unjust and does not award them. (CCP 2025.450(g)(1).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANISHIA DAVID VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Motion For Court Order To Deem Request For Admission, Set Two Admitted And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Monday, March 10, 2025, Line 7. 2 - DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion For Court Order To Deem Request For Admission, Set Two Admitted And Request For Sanctions. Defendants' motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in Chinatown Community Development Center's (CCDC) Requests for Admission, Set Two, is granted. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set Two, is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Defendants also seek sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $1000 payable by Plaintiff to CCDC. Plaintiff shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PRECILA BALABBO VS. DGL GROUP, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Monday, March 10, 2025, Line 3. PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
